canonical_name: a courtier looking respectfully at his queen, laughing and jesting with her as a privileged friend of her husband
a privileged friend of her husband
the queen, who had stepped over a stream and come away with child
any other man she had ever known
ruffians who are holding the land to ransom
the Goddess who jests cruelly with us
that husband so dear to him
that husband so dear to him that he could not lift a hand to take her
a sunlit room in Pellinore's house
the hills by the Lake, searching for the dragon
a woman in his arms, wearing Gwenhwyfar's perfume
the long arm of the sea which was the lake
